activities_projects.title: The Samanta to King Journey
activities_projects.objective: Understand how big landlords became powerful rulers
steps: Read the definition of 'Samantas' from the textbook
Identify the titles they took when they became powerful (e.g., Maha-mandaleshvara)
Describe how the Rashtrakuta chief Dantidurga became a Kshatriya through 'Hiranya-garbha'
Draw a small crown representing the shift from a sub-ordinate to a King
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the process of gaining political power through titles and rituals
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the term Samanta and the ritual Hiranya-garbha (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Prashastis: The King's Diary
activities_projects.objective: Learn what prashastis tell us about kings
steps: Read the example of Nagabhata’s prashasti in the book
Understand that these were written by Brahmanas to praise kings
Write a short 'Prashasti' for yourself, listing three brave things you have done
Note that these records may not always be 100% true
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies prashastis as a source of history and understands their purpose
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity in writing and understanding of the term 'praise' (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Tripartite Struggle Map
activities_projects.objective: Identify the three parties fighting for Kanauj
steps: Find the city of Kanauj on the map
Identify the three dynasties: Gurjara-Pratihara, Rashtrakuta, and Pala
Color the areas where these three dynasties ruled
Draw arrows from all three toward Kanauj to show the struggle
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the long-term conflict over the prize city of Kanauj
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in marking the three dynasties on the map (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sultan Mahmud’s India Visit
activities_projects.objective: Understand the impact of foreign invasions for wealth
steps: Find out which famous temple Sultan Mahmud of Ghazni raided (Somnath)
Write down what he did with the wealth he took (built a city in Ghazni)
Identify the scholar he brought with him (Al-Biruni)
Write the name of the book Al-Biruni wrote: 'Kitab-al-Hind'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the reasons for early medieval raids on Indian temples
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct facts about Mahmud of Ghazni and Al-Biruni (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Chola Temple Architecture
activities_projects.objective: Recognize the importance of temples in Chola life
materials: Pictures of Thanjavur or Gangaikonda-cholapuram temples
steps: Observe the images of the big temples built by Rajaraja and Rajendra Chola
List two things that happened at the temple (worship, dance, music, crafts)
Try to make a small model of a temple 'Vimana' (tower) using clay
Write that the temples were the 'hub' of social and economic life
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that Chola temples were not just places of worship but centers of activity
activities_projects.evaluation: Model making and understanding of the temple's multi-functional role (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Bronze Image Making
materials: Image of a Chola bronze statue (e.g., Nataraja)
steps: Look closely at a Chola bronze image in the book
Identify the technique used: 'Lost Wax' technique
Explain why Chola bronze statues are famous worldwide
Color a drawing of a statue in bronze/golden color
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes Chola bronze work as a pinnacle of medieval Indian craft
activities_projects.evaluation: Identification of the 'Lost Wax' technique (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Chola Irrigation Wonders
activities_projects.objective: Learn how the Cholas improved farming
steps: Read about the embankments and canals built in the Kaveri delta
Draw a simple diagram of a river with 'canals' branching into fields
Explain why the Cholas built 'tanks' to collect rainwater
Write how this helped in growing two crops a year
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the advanced agricultural and irrigation systems of the Cholas
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of the irrigation diagram and its benefits (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Chola Village Administration
activities_projects.objective: Understand how people lived and voted in Chola times
steps: Identify the terms: 'Ur' (settlement of peasants) and 'Nadu' (group of villages)
Read about the Uttaramerur inscription on how committees were chosen
Act out the election: Write names on palm leaves (paper), put them in a pot, and let a small boy pick them
Discuss if this was an early form of democracy
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns about the unique local self-government system of the Cholas
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct use of terms like Ur, Nadu, and Sabha (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Rajputs and Chahamanas
activities_projects.objective: Identify the brave warriors of the North
steps: Identify the 'Chahamanas' later known as the 'Chauhans'
Find out the most famous Chauhan ruler (Prithviraj III)
Identify who he defeated in 1191 (Sultan Muhammad Ghori)
Note the year he lost to the same Sultan (1192)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recalls the key rulers and battles of the Chauhan dynasty
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of dates and names related to the Chauhans (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Brahmana Land Grants
steps: Define 'Brahmadeya' (land gifted to Brahmanas)
Identify where these land grants were recorded (on copper plates)
Draw a circle representing a copper plate and write a mock 'land grant' message
Identify who looked after these lands (a Sabha or assembly)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how the king rewarded Brahmanas and how land records were kept
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of Brahmadeya and the role of copper plates (10 marks)
